div.head:
No. 12.
Mr. White to Mr. Bayard.

opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
London, April 7, 1888. (Received April 7.)

div.p: Mr. White stated that on the following Thursday he was to meet Lord Salisbury and M. de Staal to discuss the question of the protection of the seals. On April 7 he had had an interview on the subject with M. de Staal, from whom he learned that the Russian Government wished to include in the proposed arrangement that part of Behring Sea in which the Commander Islands are situated, and also the sea of Okhotsk. Mr. White supposed that the United States would not object to this.
